name: Babylon.js (3D Game Engine for the Web)

agent_readiness.notes: Full-featured 3D engine with scene graph, physics, PBR materials, animations, particle systems, GUI. Engine, Scene, Mesh, Material, Camera, Light API. Built-in physics (Havok, Cannon, Ammo). glTF/OBJ/STL import. WebXR VR/AR support. Node Material Editor (visual shader editor). Inspector debugger. Babylon Playground for live experimentation. TypeScript-first. More batteries-included than Three.js. Backed by Microsoft. Used for games, product configurators, architectural viz, metaverse. v7 adds WebGPU support.

gotchas: Larger bundle than Three.js (use tree-shaking packages @babylonjs/core etc.)
Different paradigm from Three.js — scene/mesh API, not geometry/material
Inspector adds significant size if included in production
best_when: Browser 3D games needing physics, animations, and full game engine features
Teams wanting more built-in features than Three.js
summary: Babylon.js is Microsoft's full-featured 3D game engine for browsers. More batteries-included than Three.js: built-in physics (Havok), PBR materials, particle systems, GUI, WebXR, Node Material Editor. TypeScript-first with excellent docs. v7 adds WebGPU. Used for browser games, VR/AR, product configurators. @babylonjs/core modular packages for tree-shaking. Three.js is lighter for simple 3D; Babylon.js is better for full game/simulation projects.
